user.title: Sentinel
user.description: Sentinel will start monitoring your integrations, devices and applications with sensible defaults. You can adjust all settings after setup.

data.fire_events: Fire events on the HA bus

data_description.fire_events: Fire a `sentinel_item_changed` event on the HA event bus on every state change. Required for automations.
data_description.grace_period: How long an integration must remain in a failed state before being reported (0 = immediate).
data_description.excluded_entries: Integrations to exclude from monitoring. Only the integration state is excluded — its physical devices are still monitored.
data_description.ignored_device_sources: Device sources to exclude from monitoring (e.g. mobile_app, cast). Only devices from these sources are excluded — the integration state itself is still monitored. Sources are listed from your current installation.
data_description.ignored_device_ids: Specific devices to exclude from monitoring, regardless of their source.
data_description.watch_stopped_addons: When enabled, stopped applications are reported as warnings. By default a stopped application is considered intentional.
data_description.apps_poll_interval: How often Sentinel queries the Supervisor for application states (30–300 seconds, default 60).
data_description.ignored_addon_slugs: Applications to exclude from monitoring. Useful for add-ons that are intentionally stopped (e.g. Z-Wave flasher, USB/IP client) and would otherwise trigger false alerts. The list is populated from your current installation.
